description: Sensitive-surface chain. Security-auditor runs TWICE: as advisor before executor (threat modeling + recommendations) and as gate after test-runner (validates implementation). For changes touching auth, secrets, crypto, migrations, agent/MCP config, lockfiles, GitHub workflows, permissions. Scrutiny defaults to critical.

steps.title: {{root_title}}
steps.description:
PROBLEM: (sensitive-surface change; specify auth/secrets/crypto/migration/config aspect)
SCOPE: {{scope}}
NON_GOALS: (fill in)
VALIDATION: (tests + security smoke)
ACCEPTANCE: (functional + security invariants preserved)

Sensitive surface. Security-auditor runs pre (advisor) AND post (gate). Reviewer scrutiny={{scrutiny}}; reviewer may auto-escalate further on surface table match.

steps.title: security-auditor-advisor:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: LOW-permission threat-modeling pass BEFORE executor. Identify attack surface, dependency risks, auth/secret/crypto invariants.
INPUTS: root contract; current code at {{scope}}.
OUTPUTS: structured recommendations for executor — invariants to preserve, tests to add, library/config choices to avoid. Cite CVE/CWE refs where applicable.
SCOPE: {{scope}} and adjacent security boundaries.
NON_GOALS: no edits; no exploits; recommendations only.

steps.title: executor:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: implement root's deliverable per its contract, applying security-auditor-advisor's recommendations.
INPUTS: root contract + security-auditor-advisor's structured recommendations.
OUTPUTS: diff on chain branch satisfying ACCEPTANCE; tests per VALIDATION; security invariants preserved.
SCOPE: same as root.
NON_GOALS: no `--no-verify`; no scope creep into adjacent security surfaces unless advisor flagged.

steps.title: seconder:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: fused scope/compliance + quality gate on executor's diff.
INPUTS: executor's diff; root contract.
OUTPUTS: verdict JSON with scope_verdict, scope_findings, quality_verdict, quality_findings, overall_verdict.
SCOPE: same as root.
NON_GOALS: no edits.

steps.title: test-engineer:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: write tests, fixtures, smoke scripts, and telemetry assertions against the actual diff.
INPUTS: executor's diff; seconder verdict; root contract.
OUTPUTS: tests, fixtures, smoke scripts, telemetry assertions, exact test-runner commands.
SCOPE: test paths plus diff-adjacent harness code.
NON_GOALS: no production source edits unless root explicitly authorizes helper/export change.

steps.title: test-runner:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: execute exact commands supplied by test-engineer and classify failures by owner.
INPUTS: exact commands; tests; telemetry assertions.
OUTPUTS: pass/fail counts, artifact evidence, owner routing, rerun guidance.
SCOPE: requested commands only; fallback must be labeled.
NON_GOALS: no edits; no scope expansion.

steps.title: security-auditor-gate:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: LOW-permission validation pass AFTER test-runner. Validate executor's diff against security-auditor-advisor's recommendations + general security review.
INPUTS: executor's diff; security-auditor-advisor's recommendations; test evidence.
OUTPUTS: verdict OK or FINDINGS (with severity + remediation). Reviewer treats OK as pre-condition for PASS.
SCOPE: same as root.
NON_GOALS: no edits; no exploits.

steps.title: obligations-scanner:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: scan executor's diff for unstructured markers.
INPUTS: executor's diff.
OUTPUTS: CLEAN | OBLIGATIONS_FOUND | BLOCKED.
SCOPE: same as root.
NON_GOALS: no edits.

steps.title: reviewer:{{root_title}}
steps.description:
MANDATE: compliance reviewer at SCRUTINY={{scrutiny}} (critical by default for this template).
INPUTS: executor diff + seconder verdict + security-auditor-gate verdict + obligations-scanner output + advisor recommendations + root contract.
OUTPUTS: verdict PASS|PARTIAL|FAIL + Release Checklist with security-specific items.
SCOPE: same as root.
NON_GOALS: no edits; do not waive security-auditor-gate findings.
